blurb:
If you've been putting off a body contouring consultation or keep meaning to deal with the laser hair removal situation, Brazilian Beauty Spa in East Cobb is worth a serious look.

They're a full-service medical aesthetics spa right in Marietta, specializing in body contouring, laser hair removal, skincare treatments, and general wellness. Not the kind of place that just sells you a gift card and a robe. The team skews toward clinical expertise, which means you're getting people who actually know what they're doing when it comes to the more results-oriented treatments.

What separates them from a generic day spa is the combination of beauty services and medical-grade aesthetics under one roof. You can come in for a skin consult and walk out with a real treatment plan, not just a facial and some moisturizer samples.

They're located in the heart of East Cobb in Marietta, so no hike across town required. Worth calling ahead to book since appointment windows tend to fill up.
scoring_notes: Local East Cobb business with a clear specialty and enough specifics to write to; no time-sensitive event but strong area fit

blurb:
If you've ever walked past a shop window and thought, 'okay, I need everything in there,' Market with a B. on the Marietta Square is probably that shop for you.

It's a Southern boutique that pulls together farmhouse decor, gifts, women's accessories, baby items, and what they call 'home provisions.' That last category alone says a lot about how they think about shopping. This isn't a place that sells one thing and calls it a day. The whole point is the mix: something for your shelf, something for your friend's new baby, something for yourself that you didn't know you needed until you saw it.

The curation leans toward charm and usefulness in equal measure, which is harder to pull off than it sounds. Most gift shops go heavy on one side or the other. This one seems to actually edit.

The Marietta Square location means parking can be hit or miss on weekends, but the walk from the deck is short. Worth building into a Saturday morning already.
scoring_notes: Solid local independent boutique on the Marietta Square with a clear identity; no time-sensitive event but good fit for East Cobb audience
